N3 never receives any message (no input paths to N3)

N5 receives messages only from N3 or N4 - N3 never sends, N4 never activates, so N5 never activates
Therefore: N4 and N5 never activate

T=0: N3 receives and will forward at T=1
T=0: N2 receives and forwards instantly to N4 (0-step delay)

Note: Even with instant N2 forwarding, N3's message still arrives at T=1, making T=1 the activation time
